name: Ribadesella to Mirador del Fito via Tereñes Loop
name_original: Ribadesella al Mirador del Fitu – circular por Tereñes
description: This difficult 20.9-mile mountain biking route from Ribadesella offers panoramic views from Mirador del Fitu and unique dinosaur footprints
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ribadesella offers diverse terrain where the Cantabrian Sea meets the mountains of Asturias. The region features a dramatic coastline with impressive cliffs and extensive sandy beaches, contrasting with lush green landscapes and proximity to the Picos de Europa. Riders can expect a mix of coastal paths, river valleys, and ascents into higher elevations, providing varied experiences for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Ribadesella The most popular mountain bike trail is Ribadesella to Mirador del Fito via Tereñes Loop, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to a renowned viewpoint. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ribadesella a los Acantilados del Infierno y los Acantilados de Guadamía – ruta circular, an easy 12.0 miles (19.4 km) path. This route explores the impressive coastal cliffs of Infierno and Guadamía. Local mountain bikers also love the Oreyana – Guadamía Trail, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and past the Guadamía cliffs, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Ribadesella is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, river estuaries, and green mountainous terrain. The network offers options for easy coastal rides as well as more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Ribadesella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ribadesella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella offers a diverse selection of over 25 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging ascents into the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ribadesella?
A: Yes, Ribadesella has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040925011/ribadesella-a-los-acantilados-del-infierno-y-los-acantilados-de-guadamia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella a los Acantilados del Infierno y los Acantilados de Guadamía – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12-mile loop that explores impressive coastal cliffs. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486138/oreyana-guadamia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreyana – Guadamía Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is also considered easy and takes you through beautiful coastal landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Ribadesella?
A: Experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481330/ribadesella-to-mirador-del-fito-via-terenes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella to Mirador del Fito via Tereñes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 20.9-mile trail with significant elevation gain, leading to the renowned Mirador del Fito viewpoint. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Fito – La Beciella Beach loop from Ribadesella&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 30 miles and features substantial climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ribadesella?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Ribadesella is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less rainfall than winter and cooler temperatures than the peak summer, making for ideal riding conditions. The lush green landscapes are particularly vibrant in spring.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic coastal views?
A: Absolutely! Ribadesella's dramatic coastline is a highlight for many routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040925011/ribadesella-a-los-acantilados-del-infierno-y-los-acantilados-de-guadamia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella a los Acantilados del Infierno y los Acantilados de Guadamía – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486138/oreyana-guadamia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreyana – Guadamía Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas of the Acantilados del Infierno and Guadamía Cliffs, showcasing the region's unique coastal formations.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Ribadesella?
A: Ribadesella's trails often pass by remarkable natural attractions. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Mar Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or discover geological wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuevona of Cuevas del Agua&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views from various viewpoints, including those overlooking the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that are circular?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Ribadesella are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481330/ribadesella-to-mirador-del-fito-via-terenes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella to Mirador del Fito via Tereñes Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040925011/ribadesella-a-los-acantilados-del-infierno-y-los-acantilados-de-guadamia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella a los Acantilados del Infierno y los Acantilados de Guadamía – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, both offering convenient circular journeys.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ribadesella?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ribadesella are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, from dramatic coastal cliffs to lush green mountainous landscapes, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that lead to significant viewpoints?
A: Yes, several trails offer access to spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481330/ribadesella-to-mirador-del-fito-via-terenes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella to Mirador del Fito via Tereñes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you to the renowned Mirador del Fito, offering panoramic views of the Sueve Nature Reserve, Ponga Natural Park, and the Picos de Europa. The Ardines area also provides excellent views of Ribadesella itself.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that combine coastal and mountain scenery?
A: Ribadesella is uniquely positioned where the sea meets the mountains, allowing for routes that seamlessly blend both landscapes. You can enjoy coastal paths with views of the Cantabrian Sea and then transition into green, mountainous terrain, sometimes even with glimpses of the Picos de Europa in the distance. This combination is a defining feature of mountain biking in the region.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical or cultural landmarks?
A: While primarily focused on natural beauty, some routes in Ribadesella may intersect with historical elements. The region is part of the Northern or Coastal Way of St. James, so you might encounter sections of this historic pilgrimage route. Additionally, the Grúa Walk along the River Sella estuary offers a journey through history with ceramic murals.
tips: On one of the most famous hills in the Sueve mountain range stands an old concrete watchtower from the early 20th century: the Mirador del Fito. From here, we can capture a seamless, almost blink-and-you-miss sequence shot that immediately reveals the basic elements of the Asturian landscape: sea, mid-mountains, high mountains, meadows, valleys, fog, and towns.

The granaries of Asturias are characteristic constructions of the countryside that served to protect the harvest from humidity. Although very similar in appearance to the Galician version, the Asturian hórreo is usually square (the ones in Galicia are usually rectangular) and built of wood (the Galician ones are usually made of stone).

By moving over sandy or muddy sediments, dinosaurs have made series of impressions. When these traces are preserved over time fossilized in a layer, they are called "footprints". If you want to look for the footprints, remember to always do the route at low tide. You also have to be careful with the rocks – except on sunny days, they are usually wet or printed with algae. In the town of Tereñes there is an explanatory panel. The three groups that can be most clearly identified are marked by plates that reproduce the footprint of a theropod.

This old lighthouse came into operation in 1861, with a fixed white light, which varied by flashes of four in four minutes, using fuel for its operation. Since 2008 it has been used for cultural purposes. It offers simply spectacular views that you will not be able to forget.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route usually takes around 3 hours and 11 minutes to complete. However, given its 'difficult' rating and technical sections (STS S3-S6), your actual duration may vary depending on your skill level and how often you stop to enjoy the views or navigate difficult terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly demanding, rated STS S3-S6. This means you'll encounter blocked singletracks with large boulders, root passages, and very steep sections. Expect loose surfaces, stones, and potentially tight switchbacks. Riders should be prepared for sections where pushing or carrying the bike might be necessary due to extreme steepness or technical obstacles.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the Ribadesella al Mirador del Fitu route?
faqs.answer: This route is packed with incredible sights. You'll experience breathtaking panoramic views from the iconic Mirador del Fito, stretching from the Cantabrian Sea to the Picos de Europa. Along the coastal section near Tereñes, you can discover unique Dinosaur Tracks at Tereñes Cliffs. Other highlights include the Ribadesella Lighthouse and an Asturian Hórreo.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners or families looking for a leisurely ride. It requires very good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills, with technical sections rated STS S3-S6. Riders should be prepared for challenging ascents and potentially pushing their bikes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ribadesella al Mirador del Fitu mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Ribadesella. You should be able to find parking options within Ribadesella itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular trail. Specific parking areas are not detailed, so it's advisable to look for public parking upon arrival in the town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: To fully enjoy the spectacular views and more favorable riding conditions, the best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring or autumn months. Summer can also be good, but be mindful of potential heat and crowds. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially given the trail's challenging nature and exposure at viewpoints like Mirador del Fito.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular mountain bike route are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in Asturias. However, given the challenging and technical nature of this route, and the presence of other users, it is highly recommended to keep dogs on a leash for their safety and the safety of others. Be prepared for sections where you might need to dismount or carry your bike, which could be difficult with a dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the 'Ribadesella al Mirador del Fitu – circular por Tereñes' mountain bike trail. It is a public route accessible to all. However, always respect local regulations and private property along the way.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Ruta entre playas, the Northern Route (Asturia), and the Mtb Northern Route (Asturia). Smaller segments also overlap with [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Les Arriondes, [CIMA AS24] El Fito * Loroñe, Way of St. James (northern cyclist route), Camin Real, Rampa 7, and Camin de la Espinera.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and technical terrain, you should bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical trails, a helmet, gloves, and protective eyewear. Carry plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Layered clothing is advisable, as weather conditions can change, especially at higher elevations like Mirador del Fito.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a natural trail, especially in the more mountainous sections. While Ribadesella at the start/end offers various amenities, and there might be small villages or establishments near the Mirador del Fito, it's best to assume limited access to cafes or reliable water sources directly on the trail itself. Always carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of your ride.
